milestones.title: Phase 1 - World/Persona Modeling
milestones.description: Produce a stable, evidence-backed world model and persona layer for the Fog Harbor demo.

milestones.title: Phase 2 - Scenario/Simulation Loop
milestones.description: Stabilize baseline/intervention simulation, trace, report, and eval loops.

milestones.title: Phase 3 - Eval/UI/Demo
milestones.description: Finish the workbench, final demo loop, and review-grade presentation layer.

milestones.title: Phase 4 - Review Workflow and Ops Hardening
milestones.description: Restart the GitHub execution queue, harden successor-phase automation, and upgrade the workbench into a review workflow without expanding core simulation/report contracts.

milestones.title: Phase 5 - Review Sign-off and Evidence Packaging
milestones.description: Turn the Phase 4 review workflow into a usable sign-off flow, package review output for handoff, and remove repeated successor-queue bootstrap friction without expanding simulation or artifact contracts.

milestones.title: Phase 6 - Automation Activation and Queue Hygiene
milestones.description: Resume the long-running loop by activating local Codex queue automation on top of the worktree runbook, syncing repo truth to the new queue, and cleaning historical branch noise without changing simulation or artifact contracts.

milestones.title: Phase 7 - Operator Handoff and Review Delivery
milestones.description: Turn the resumed queue into a reviewer-to-operator handoff loop by adding GitHub-ready delivery surfaces in the workbench without changing core simulation or artifact contracts.

labels.name: phase:1
labels.description: Phase 1 world and persona modeling work.

labels.name: phase:3
labels.description: Phase 3 UI, eval, and demo work.

labels.name: phase:4
labels.description: Phase 4 review workflow and ops hardening work.

labels.name: phase:5
labels.description: Phase 5 review sign-off and evidence packaging work.

labels.name: phase:6
labels.description: Phase 6 automation activation and queue hygiene work.

labels.name: phase:7
labels.description: Phase 7 operator handoff and review delivery work.

labels.name: area:backend
labels.description: Backend, CLI, contracts, or artifact pipeline work.

labels.name: area:docs-evals
labels.description: Docs, ADRs, rubrics, or eval-harness work.

labels.name: risk:core-contract
labels.description: Touches protected-core contracts and cannot auto-merge.

labels.name: risk:safety
labels.description: Touches safety, redlines, or policy boundaries.

labels.name: risk:ci
labels.description: Touches CI, automation, or workflow reliability.

labels.name: lane:auto-safe
labels.description: Eligible for autonomous safe-lane execution and merge.

labels.name: lane:protected-core
labels.description: Protected-core lane; may be automated but must not auto-merge.

labels.name: status:ready
labels.description: Ready for Codex triage or execution.

labels.name: status:blocked
labels.description: Blocked and should not be picked by builder automation.

labels.name: status:needs-adr
labels.description: Needs an ADR or contract decision before merge.

issues.title: Day 0 bootstrap: create a clean automation baseline
issues.milestone: Phase 1 - World/Persona Modeling
issues.body:
## goal
Create the clean baseline required before long-running automation can write code or auto-merge.

## input
- Current repository baseline state
- Existing Phase 0/Phase 1 repo state
- Automation plan in docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md

## output
- A clean baseline snapshot or baseline PR
- Confirmed milestones and label taxonomy
- CI upgraded from phase0-only checks to long-running automation gates

## out-of-scope
- Building new product features
- Expanding the simulation contract

## minimal test
- `git -c safe.directory=D:/mirror status --short`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes automation governance and merge policy.

## phase
Phase 1 bootstrap gate

issues.title: Phase 1 exit gate
issues.milestone: Phase 1 - World/Persona Modeling
issues.body:
## goal
Validate that all Phase 1 exit criteria are met before automation advances to Phase 2.

## input
- Current artifacts/demo outputs
- Current Phase 1 milestone state
- ADR and contract notes

## output
- Phase audit summary
- Gap issues if anything is missing
- Go/no-go decision for Phase 2

## out-of-scope
- Implementing new features unrelated to exit criteria

## minimal test
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase1`

## touches contract
Yes. Exit gating can block or permit phase progression.

## phase
Phase 1

issues.title: Phase 2 exit gate
issues.milestone: Phase 2 - Scenario/Simulation Loop
issues.body:
## goal
Verify Phase 2 exit criteria before the automation system opens Phase 3.

## input
- run/report/eval artifacts
- open protected-core PR state
- milestone completion state

## output
- phase audit summary
- explicit go/no-go decision
- follow-up gap issues when needed

## out-of-scope
- new scenario work beyond unmet criteria

## minimal test
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase2`

## touches contract
Yes. Exit gates are protected-core.

## phase
Phase 2

issues.title: Phase 3 exit gate
issues.milestone: Phase 3 - Eval/UI/Demo
issues.body:
## goal
Confirm that all blueprint Phase 3 criteria are satisfied and the automation system can pause permanently.

## input
- final artifacts and frontend workbench
- current milestone and PR state
- human review rubric output

## output
- final completion report
- PAUSED automation state
- documented stop condition

## out-of-scope
- follow-on enterprise productization

## minimal test
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase3`

## touches contract
Yes. This issue controls the project stop condition.

## phase
Phase 3

issues.title: Phase 4 exit gate
issues.milestone: Phase 4 - Review Workflow and Ops Hardening
issues.body:
## goal
Confirm that the Phase 4 review workflow and ops hardening criteria are satisfied before the automation system advances again.

## input
- Phase 4 milestone state
- merged PR state for bootstrap/governance and workbench review workflow work
- local validation commands and reviewed artifacts

## output
- explicit Phase 4 closeout decision
- documented stop condition for the Phase 4 queue
- gap issues if any execution issue remains incomplete

## out-of-scope
- opening the next successor milestone
- engine/schema/report contract expansion beyond the approved Phase 4 slice

## minimal test
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase3`

## touches contract
Yes. Exit gating controls whether the automation queue may advance.

## phase
Phase 4

issues.title: Phase 4: harden successor-milestone bootstrap and builder pause/resume rules
issues.milestone: Phase 4 - Review Workflow and Ops Hardening
issues.body:
## goal
Harden successor-milestone bootstrap and builder pause/resume rules so post-Phase-3 work is governed by executable repo rules instead of docs-only convention.

## input
- `.github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json`
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/work-item.yml`
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/phase-exit.yml`
- `backend/app/automation/service.py`
- current post-Phase-3 handoff docs

## output
- `phase:4` added to repo taxonomy and bootstrap spec
- issue templates updated to support Phase 4
- local automation/CLI rule that treats missing open successor milestone or exit gate as paused-by-rule
- docs aligned to the hardened successor-phase flow

## out-of-scope
- changing simulation/report/claim/evidence/scenario contracts
- building product-facing review workflow UI

## minimal test
- `python scripts/bootstrap_github.py --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`
- `python -m backend.app.cli classify-lane --files .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/work-item.yml .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/phase-exit.yml .github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json backend/app/automation/service.py`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase3`

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes automation governance and successor-phase execution rules.

## phase
Phase 4

issues.title: Phase 4: add claim -> evidence drill-down in the workbench
issues.milestone: Phase 4 - Review Workflow and Ops Hardening
issues.body:
## goal
Add claim -> evidence drill-down in the workbench so reviewers can move from a claim card to the supporting corpus, graph, scenario, and trace context without leaving the UI.

## input
- `artifacts/demo/report/claims.json`
- `artifacts/demo/ingest/chunks.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/ingest/documents.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/graph/graph.json`
- `artifacts/demo/scenario/*.json`
- `artifacts/demo/run/*/run_trace.jsonl`
- current `frontend/src/app/page.tsx`

## output
- actionable claim review panel in the workbench
- evidence drill-down using existing artifact files only
- no backend API expansion and no artifact schema changes

## out-of-scope
- editing or regenerating artifacts from the browser
- changing report, claim, scenario, or run-trace contracts

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`

## touches contract
No. This issue must stay frontend-only and consume current artifacts unchanged.

## phase
Phase 4

issues.title: Phase 4: add baseline/intervention trace timeline in the workbench
issues.milestone: Phase 4 - Review Workflow and Ops Hardening
issues.body:
## goal
Add a baseline/intervention trace timeline in the workbench so reviewers can compare branch evolution turn by turn using existing run artifacts.

## input
- `artifacts/demo/run/baseline/run_trace.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/run/reporter_detained/run_trace.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/run/*/snapshots/*.json`
- `artifacts/demo/report/claims.json`
- current workbench frontend

## output
- reviewer-readable branch timeline and turn comparison view
- timeline links that connect related claim turns to baseline/intervention run context
- no new derived artifact files and no simulation output changes in the first slice

## out-of-scope
- changing simulation semantics
- adding new backend endpoints or new persistent artifact formats

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`

## touches contract
No. This issue must remain a UI-only consumer of existing run artifacts.

## phase
Phase 4

## phase
Phase 5

issues.title: Phase 5: decouple successor bootstrap from hardcoded phase templates and sync queue docs
issues.milestone: Phase 5 - Review Sign-off and Evidence Packaging
issues.body:
## goal
Remove repeated successor-phase bootstrap friction by decoupling phase handling from hardcoded issue form/template enumerations and syncing the queue docs to the new active Phase 5 state.

## input
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/work-item.yml`
- `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/phase-exit.yml`
- `.github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json`
- queue state docs under `docs/plans/`
- current `audit-github-queue` behavior

## output
- issue/template handling no longer requires a manual hardcoded phase dropdown patch for each successor phase
- bootstrap metadata and queue docs aligned with Phase 5 as the only active execution milestone
- protected-core governance note for how future phases should be introduced

## out-of-scope
- simulation, report, claim, evidence, scenario, or artifact contract changes
- new frontend review features

## minimal test
- `python scripts/bootstrap_github.py --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`
- `python -m backend.app.cli classify-lane --files .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/work-item.yml .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/phase-exit.yml .github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md docs/plans/phase-execution-queue.md`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-github-queue --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes queue governance and external GitHub intake behavior.

## phase
Phase 5

issues.title: Phase 5: add reviewer scorecard and sign-off worksheet in the workbench
issues.milestone: Phase 5 - Review Sign-off and Evidence Packaging
issues.body:
## goal
Turn the static human review rubric into an actionable reviewer scorecard and sign-off worksheet inside the workbench.

## input
- `docs/rubrics/human-review.md`
- `artifacts/demo/report/claims.json`
- `artifacts/demo/eval/summary.json`
- `artifacts/demo/run/*/run_trace.jsonl`
- current `frontend/src/app/page.tsx`

## output
- reviewer-facing 1-5 score controls for Usefulness, Credibility, Explainability, and Actionability
- sign-off worksheet summary and reviewer notes region generated directly in the workbench
- frontend-only derived state that still consumes existing artifacts unchanged

## out-of-scope
- backend APIs, persistent reviewer storage, or new artifact files
- changes to report schema, claim labels, or evidence semantics

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- manual review that worksheet text updates when rubric scores change

## touches contract
No. This issue must remain frontend-only and artifact-read-only.

## phase
Phase 5

issues.title: Phase 5: add shareable review packet export from claims, timeline, and rubric
issues.milestone: Phase 5 - Review Sign-off and Evidence Packaging
issues.body:
## goal
Add a shareable review packet export so a reviewer can package claims, divergent turns, eval summary, and rubric context into a reusable handoff artifact without leaving the workbench.

## input
- `artifacts/demo/report/claims.json`
- `artifacts/demo/run/baseline/run_trace.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/run/reporter_detained/run_trace.jsonl`
- `artifacts/demo/eval/summary.json`
- `docs/rubrics/human-review.md`
- current workbench frontend

## output
- a copyable Markdown review packet derived entirely in the frontend
- exported content that includes claim IDs, turn IDs, rubric dimensions, and current summary context
- no backend export endpoint and no new persistent artifact format in this slice

## out-of-scope
- posting directly to GitHub
- changing report or trace schemas
- generating new saved files under `artifacts/`

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- manual review that exported Markdown includes claim IDs and turn IDs

## touches contract
No. This issue must remain a frontend-only consumer of the current artifact tree.

## phase
Phase 5

issues.title: Phase 5: codify worktree-based orchestrator pickup and handoff runbook
issues.milestone: Phase 5 - Review Sign-off and Evidence Packaging
issues.body:
## goal
Codify the worktree-based orchestrator pickup and handoff runbook so long-running execution no longer depends on oral convention.

## input
- `docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md`
- `docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md`
- `docs/plans/phase-execution-queue.md`
- active GitHub Phase 5 queue state
- `audit-github-queue` and phase audit commands

## output
- `docs/plans/long-running-loop-runbook.md`
- a documented pickup order for authenticated queue audit, worktree naming, one-writer issue ownership, lane-specific review paths, and post-merge re-audit
- explicit branch hygiene rules for historical `origin/codex/*` branches

## out-of-scope
- local automation cards, heartbeat schedules, or cron setup
- new worktree wrapper scripts
- simulation, report, or artifact contract changes

## minimal test
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-github-queue --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`
- `python -m backend.app.cli classify-lane --files docs/plans/long-running-loop-runbook.md docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md docs/plans/phase-execution-queue.md`
- manual review that the runbook can drive pickup -> worktree -> PR -> checkpoint without unstated steps

## touches contract
Yes. This work defines the operational execution surface for long-running queue consumption.

## phase
Phase 5

issues.title: Phase 6 exit gate
issues.milestone: Phase 6 - Automation Activation and Queue Hygiene
issues.body:
## goal
Confirm that the Phase 6 automation activation and queue hygiene criteria are satisfied before the automation system advances again.

## input
- Phase 6 milestone state
- merged PR state for queue sync, automation activation, and branch hygiene work
- local validation commands and reviewed artifacts

## output
- explicit Phase 6 closeout decision
- documented stop condition for the Phase 6 queue
- gap issues if any execution issue remains incomplete

## out-of-scope
- opening the next successor milestone before Phase 6 completion
- simulation, report, claim, evidence, scenario, or artifact contract expansion

## minimal test
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-phase phase3`
- `python -m backend.app.cli audit-github-queue --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`

## touches contract
Yes. Exit gating controls whether the automation queue may advance.

## phase
Phase 6

issues.title: Phase 6: sync bootstrap spec and docs to the active automation queue
issues.milestone: Phase 6 - Automation Activation and Queue Hygiene
issues.body:
## goal
Sync the repository source of truth from the paused post-Phase-5 baseline to the active Phase 6 queue so docs, bootstrap metadata, and README reflect the newly resumed long-running loop.

## input
- `.github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json`
- `README.md`
- `docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md`
- `docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md`
- `docs/plans/phase-execution-queue.md`
- current live GitHub milestone and issue state

## output
- `phase:6` and Phase 6 queue objects recorded in the bootstrap spec
- README and planning docs updated to show Phase 6 as the active successor queue
- no stale Phase 5 paused-baseline language remains in the active-state docs

## out-of-scope
- local automation card creation
- remote branch deletion
- simulation, report, or artifact contract changes

## minimal test
- `python scripts/bootstrap_github.py --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`
- `python -m backend.app.cli classify-lane --files .github/automation/bootstrap-spec.json README.md docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md docs/plans/phase-execution-queue.md`
- `./make.ps1 test`
- authenticated `python -m backend.app.cli audit-github-queue --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes the active operational GitHub queue truth surface.

## phase
Phase 6

issues.title: Phase 6: define and activate local Codex queue heartbeat against the worktree runbook
issues.milestone: Phase 6 - Automation Activation and Queue Hygiene
issues.body:
## goal
Define and activate the first local Codex queue heartbeat against the worktree runbook so the long-running loop can resume from a real recurring operator surface instead of a doc-only manual path.

## input
- `docs/plans/long-running-loop-runbook.md`
- `docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md`
- current authenticated `audit-github-queue` behavior
- current Codex desktop automation capabilities

## output
- one documented local automation configuration for queue heartbeat / pickup review
- operator instructions for pause, resume, and failure handling
- no repo scheduler code and no daemon process inside the repository

## out-of-scope
- simulation/report/artifact contract changes
- branch deletion
- backend service endpoints for automation state

## minimal test
- manual verification that the automation definition points at the correct workspace and queue audit command
- authenticated `python -m backend.app.cli audit-github-queue --repo YSCJRH/mirror-sim`

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes the live execution posture of the long-running loop.

## phase
Phase 6

issues.title: Phase 6: classify and clean superseded remote codex branches
issues.milestone: Phase 6 - Automation Activation and Queue Hygiene
issues.body:
## goal
Apply the documented branch hygiene rules to the visible historical `origin/codex/*` branches so the resumed queue is not obscured by stale remote branch noise.

## input
- `docs/plans/long-running-loop-runbook.md`
- `docs/plans/current-state-baseline.md`
- current remote branch inventory
- current open issues and PR state

## output
- a classified inventory of historical remote branches
- deletion of clearly superseded remote branches that are no longer referenced by open work
- an explicit keep/revive note for any branches that must remain

## out-of-scope
- reopening historical work without a new issue
- deleting branches that are still tied to open work or unresolved forensic comparison
- simulation/report/artifact contract changes

## minimal test
- `git branch -r`
- `gh api repos/YSCJRH/mirror-sim/branches?per_page=100`
- manual verification that no kept branch is still undocumented

## touches contract
Yes. This work changes the operational GitHub hygiene surface for the long-running loop.

## phase
Phase 6

Phase 7

## goal
Sync the repository source of truth from the paused post-Phase-6 baseline to the active Phase 7 queue so docs, bootstrap metadata, and README reflect the newly resumed handoff loop.

## input

- `README.md`
- `docs/plans/automation-roadmap.md`

## out-of-scope

## minimal test

## phase
Phase 7

issues.title: Phase 7: add issue-comment-ready review packet sections in the workbench
issues.milestone: Phase 7 - Operator Handoff and Review Delivery
issues.body:
## goal
Extend the workbench review packet into an issue-comment-ready handoff surface so an operator can copy a GitHub-ready review brief without reformatting the current packet by hand.

## input
- current `frontend/src/app/review-scorecard.tsx`
- current claim packet, divergent turn packet, and reviewer note state
- existing demo artifacts under `artifacts/demo/**`

## output
- copyable issue-comment-ready markdown sections in the workbench
- handoff copy that preserves claim IDs, divergent turn IDs, reviewer notes, and sign-off posture
- no backend API calls and no persistent artifact writes

## out-of-scope
- posting directly to GitHub
- changing report, trace, or rubric contracts
- introducing repo-side automation logic

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- manual review that the copied packet reads cleanly as a GitHub issue or PR comment

## touches contract
No. This issue must remain frontend-only and artifact-read-only.

## phase
Phase 7

issues.title: Phase 7: add decision brief and next-action handoff panel in the workbench
issues.milestone: Phase 7 - Operator Handoff and Review Delivery
issues.body:
## goal
Add a decision brief and next-action handoff panel to the workbench so the reviewer can package a recommended next step, blockers, and sign-off posture for the next operator pickup.

## input
- current reviewer scorecard and notes state
- current packet export state
- existing eval summary and claim/timeline context from the Phase 5 workbench

## output
- a handoff-focused decision brief panel in the workbench
- structured next-action / blocker / recommendation sections derived from existing frontend state
- no backend API calls and no new artifact files

## out-of-scope
- live GitHub issue mutation
- simulation, report, rubric, or queue contract changes
- local automation schedule changes

## minimal test
- `npm run build --prefix frontend`
- `./make.ps1 smoke`
- `./make.ps1 eval-demo`
- manual review that the brief can be copied as an operator handoff summary without editing raw JSON

## touches contract
No. This issue must remain frontend-only and artifact-read-only.

## phase
Phase 7
